description: Evaluation cases for computational model peer review readiness skill

evals.prompt: Peer review my computational model repository for CoMSES readiness and give a final decision.
evals.expected_output: Structured report that evaluates ease of execution, documentation thoroughness, and code quality as required criteria, then returns a binary pass/fail recommendation
success_criteria: Output includes all three CoMSES criteria with explicit ratings
Final recommendation is binary (Pass or Fail)
Report includes a required criteria status summary showing 3/3 required

evals.prompt: Can you review the scientific novelty of my model and decide if the theory is correct?
evals.expected_output: Skill activates but explicitly scopes review to software and documentation quality, noting scientific merit is out of scope

evals.prompt: Review this model package. Tests are missing and CI is not configured, but it runs and has strong docs and clean code.
evals.expected_output: Report flags tests and CI as major nonblocking issues and still applies binary pass/fail from the three CoMSES required criteria
success_criteria: Tests and CI are categorized as Major findings
Missing tests/CI alone does not automatically force Fail
Binary decision is based on required CoMSES criteria

evals.prompt: Assess my model documentation. I use a framework equivalent to ODD, not ODD itself.
evals.expected_output: Documentation is evaluated for equivalent coverage rather than failing solely for non-ODD format
success_criteria: Report checks purpose, entities/components, processes, assumptions, parameters, initialization, inputs/outputs, and stochasticity where relevant
Equivalent framework is accepted when coverage is sufficient

evals.prompt: I have ODD docs. Use the peer-review process to assess whether the documentation passes quality checks.
evals.expected_output: Review references ODD-oriented assessment and indicates use of the document skill for ODD completeness/checklist support
success_criteria: Output indicates handoff or reference to the document skill for ODD checklist evaluation

evals.prompt: Write a Python script to parse CSV files into JSON.
evals.expected_output: Skill does not activate; task is generic programming and not peer review

evals.prompt: Create a conference abstract about my simulation findings.
evals.expected_output: Skill does not activate; task is scientific writing, not computational model peer review

evals.prompt: Run a peer-review readiness assessment and explicitly anchor your findings to the local compressed CoMSES and EVERSE rubric snapshots.
evals.expected_output: Structured review output cites the local compressed rubric assets and uses them as evidence anchors in the report
success_criteria: Output references assets/COMSES-REVIEWS-COMPRESSED.md for CoMSES criteria grounding
Output references assets/EVERSE-INDICATORS-COMPRESSED.md for indicator grounding
Output still applies binary required-criteria pass/fail logic for the three CoMSES criteria
